summary: Add CoordinationLift as 11th analysis dimension — the core metric for Twining benchmarks
context: Plan review revealed the Python analysis package was missing CoordinationLift analysis — the delta between coordinated and uncoordinated agent performance. This is the project's most important metric. The TS codebase already has CoordinationLift and StandaloneScoreResult types.
rationale: CoordinationLift directly answers "does coordination help?" which is the benchmark's raison d'etre. Without it, the analysis package would miss the primary signal. Added as Task 9g with full implementation including pairwise lift, per-scenario breakdown, per-dimension breakdown, and summary with significance testing.

alternatives.option: Fold coordination lift into the existing conditions analyzer
alternatives.reason_rejected: Conditions analyzer focuses on pairwise effect sizes between all conditions. CoordinationLift has distinct semantics — it classifies conditions as coordinated vs uncoordinated and measures the lift specifically from coordination tools. Deserves its own dimension.
